zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne Wood於1477年出生在英國戴文易亞斯特 沃林頓時，她的父親Sir Thomas Wood JR32歲，母親Margaret de la Mare21歲。她於大約1495年與Sir Thomas Stuckley在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1560年在她的家鄉享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Anne Wood于1477年出生在英国戴文易亞斯特 沃林頓时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Wood JR32岁，母亲Margaret de la Mare21岁。她于大约1495年与Sir Thomas Stuckley在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1560年在她的家乡享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: mainly a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a wood, from Middle English wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as French Bois and Polish Les .
English: in a few cases, a nickname for an eccentric or perhaps a violent person, from Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd).

variants.name: Woode
meanings: English: variant of   Wood  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 312, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Woods
meanings: English: variant of Wood with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish: adopted as a translation of Ó Cuill ‘descendant of Coll’ (see Quill ), or in Ulster of Mac Con Coille ‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’, which has also been mistranslated Cox , as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne Wood was born in 1477, in East Worlington, Devon, England, her father, Sir Thomas Wood JR, was 32 and her mother, Margaret de la Mare, was 21. She married Sir Thomas Stuckley about 1495, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1560, in her hometown, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Anne Wood est née en 1477, à East Worlington, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Wood JR, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Margaret de la Mare, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Stuckley environ 1495, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1560, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Wood(이)가 1477년 영국 데폰 이스트 워링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Wood JR(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de la Mare(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1495년 영국 데폰에서 Sir Thomas Stuckley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1560년 그녀의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Anne Wood родилась в 1477 г. в местности эаст ворлингтон, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Wood JR было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de la Mare, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Stuckley около 1495 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1560 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 83 лет.
